detail: FULL golden suite (test_golden.py) = 1181 passed / 1088 xfailed / 0 failed in 105s, production mode, at committed HEAD fc7017f900. Gate reported 1-cell regression (1180/1181) but NOT reproduced locally. Large-D suspects (128x512,128x1024, batched n up to 64, KV_DEPTH=1) all pass. Batching is ablation-proven zero-win (compute-bound). Regression is intermittent(race) or environmental. Escalating to static analyzer on batch=true path before deciding fix-vs-park.

description: Structural review of batched SDPA kernels

analysis: Static analyzer: batch=true path structurally correct + byte-identical to per-tile for all supported shapes (full-slot slot-aligned non-straddling reserves; counts balance; no deadlock cycle; get_tile_size==buffer_page_size for all supported dtypes). Regression not a structural batch bug -> rare bursty-NoC transient stall on silicon OR infra flake. Batching is ablation-proven zero-win (compute-bound). Decision: PARK the DM-batching knob at per-tile trivial default (byte-identical to gate-passing R2), keep read_tiles/write_tiles scaffolding as live knob for R3a. Guarantees gate bullet-3 no-regression + bullet-1 no-hang.

detail: After parking DM-batching (batch=false): core unit tests 58 passed (acceptance test_scaled_dot_product_attention + nonaligned + coarse_chunk + debug). Reader/writer now byte-identical to R2 per-tile.
